mnemonics: Half-Adder: Half the story (no carry-in). Full-Adder: Full story (has carry-in and carry-out).
Subtraction is just Addition: To subtract, add the two's complement. Flip the bits and add one!
Overflow Overflow: When the sign bits lie. If you add two positives and get a negative, you've overflowed.
keyPoints: A Binary Adder-Subtractor circuit uses a basic Full-Adder as its core building block.
The mode input (M) controls the operation: M=0 for addition, M=1 for subtraction.

Subtraction is performed by adding the two's complement of the subtrahend (B). The XOR gate acts as a controlled inverter when M=1 to create the one's complement, and the carry-in (C_in) is set to M to complete the two's complement by adding 1.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to set the initial carry-in (C0) to 1 for the subtraction operation, which is the '+1' in the two's complement.
Ignoring overflow. The circuit can produce an incorrect result due to overflow, which must be detected by examining the carry into and out of the most significant bit.
Confusing the roles of the inputs. The value fed into the XOR gates is the B input, not the A input, when creating the one's complement for subtraction.
